channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, ABC 4

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Death of 90s sitcom actress being investigated by homicide detectives: report
item.description: Per TMZ, the woman has been identified as 58-year-old actress Cindyana Santangelo, best known for a role in "Married With Children.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WGN 9

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender teens fight back against Trump's ban on participation in girls' sports
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Parker Tirrell, 16, enjoys her art classes, scrolling on TikTok and working at her new job at a pet store. But most of all, the transgender teen loves playing soccer. Until last year, that wasn’t a problem.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Texas city almost entirely underwater amid rain storms
item.description: Nearly all of the city of Rio Hondo is underwater after the massive rain storms that hit the Rio Grande Valley on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Diana Eva Maldonado, NewsNation

item.title: California Pizza Kitchen celebrates 40 years with throwback menu and prizes
item.description: California Pizza Kitchen is celebrating its 40th anniversary with a series of promotions, returning menu items and a collaboration with actor and former employee actress Busy Philipps.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving WWII paratrooper, has died
item.description: Sgt. Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving World War II paratrooper and a member of the U.S. Army’s first all-Black parachute infantry battalion, has died. He was 108. Harris died March 15 in a hospital in Los Angeles surrounded by family, grandson Ashton Pittman told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A look at who has been detained or deported in a US crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: Supporters of Palestinian causes with ties to American universities have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: American couple still imprisoned in Mexico over timeshare dispute
item.description: A Michigan couple remains behind bars in a Mexican prison, accused of breaching a timeshare contract — but their lawyer said the resort that filed the criminal complaint against them breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: The US is hunting for eggs in Europe
item.description: It is common in parts of Europe for consumers to buy eggs that still have feathers and chicken poop stuck to them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poll of scientists says large majority weighing leaving US
item.description: The scientific journal Nature poll found that 75.3 percent of scientists are considering leaving the U.S. after the administration cut funding for research.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A string of crashes this year along with a near miss have brought attention to air travel, which remains overwhelmingly safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter detained by feds
item.description: A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court won't halt order barring Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic journalist says Trump officials included him in war plans group chat
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, editor in chief of The Atlantic, was apparently included in a Trump administration group chat on Signal in which top officials debated and then discussed details of attacks against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
